hero.title: Bitcoin
hero.subtitle: Global Settlement Layer
hero.description: Decentralized infrastructure for transferring value. Open, permissionless, always running—like email, but for money.

layer1.name: Base Layer
layer1.desc: Settlement & finality. Immutable ledger secured by proof-of-work.

layer2.name: Lightning Network
layer2.desc: Instant payments at scale. Millions of txs/sec off-chain.

layer3.name: Application Layer
layer3.desc: Wallets, services, and protocols built on top.

partners.title: Buy Bitcoin in Mexico
partners.subtitle: Your safe path to financial freedom
partners.intro: Bitcoin is digital money you control. In Mexico, you can buy it using your bank (SPEI) or card.

sponsor.name: Aureo
sponsor.description: Aureo is the Mexican platform that makes your first step into Bitcoin easy. Buy with SPEI in seconds, withdraw via Lightning Network, and learn while building your financial future. Made with love in Mexico, for Mexicans. 💛

partners.footer: 💛 Aureo supports Bitcoin education in Mexico • Thanks for trusting them
partners.newPointer: Is it your first time? Ask in the chat: 'How do I buy my first Bitcoin?' 🤖✨

glossary.lightning: Instant Bitcoin payments, ideal for small amounts.

markets.title: Network Metrics
markets.subtitle: Market data is secondary to network utility

chat.title: Talk to Bitcoin Agent
chat.subtitle: Ask anything: what Bitcoin is, how to buy it, why it matters. I explain everything in plain language. What can I help you with?
chat.placeholder: Ask anything: What is Bitcoin? How do I buy?
chat.welcome:
Hi! I'm your guide to learning Bitcoin from scratch. You don't need any prior knowledge. You can ask me things like:

• What is Bitcoin and why does it matter?
• How does it protect me from inflation?
• What is a "seed phrase" and why is it so important?
• How do I send money to my family cheaper than Western Union?
• How do I buy Bitcoin in Mexico?

Pick one or type your own question.
chat.error: Failed to get response. Please try again.
suggestedQuestions: What is Bitcoin in simple words?
Why Bitcoin for someone in Mexico?
How do I buy Bitcoin with my bank?
How does it protect me from inflation?

tip.thankYou: Thank you for keeping the orange flame alive!

footer.missionText: Learn Bitcoin by playing with it. Interactive educational platform with AI tutor, practical labs, and real Lightning Network. No sign-ups. No tracking. Just pure Bitcoin education.

about.title: About This Project
about.subtitle: Bitcoin education, made interactive and accessible for Mexico.

team.cdmx: Architecture, features, and i18n unification. Making Bitcoin education beautiful and functional.
team.merida: Noob-friendly fork, UI polish, and Spanish-first UX. Making Bitcoin accessible to everyone.

seedLab.title: Seed Phrase Lab
seedLab.subtitle: Understand the most important 12-24 words of your Bitcoin journey
seedLab.introText: In Bitcoin, you control your money. There is no bank to recover your password. Twelve to twenty-four words (called a "seed phrase") are like the master key to all your Bitcoin. If you lose them or someone sees them, you lose everything. This lab teaches you what they are, how they are created, and how to protect them.
seedLab.glossarySeedPhrase: The 12-24 words that control all your Bitcoin. Like a master key—whoever has them controls your money.
seedLab.glossaryEntropy: Random numbers that make your phrase impossible to guess. The foundation of your security.
seedLab.glossaryChecksum: Extra data that helps detect if you wrote a word wrong when restoring.
seedLab.glossary11bits: Each 11-bit group maps to one of 2048 words (2^11 = 2048). This makes your seed readable but still secure!
seedLab.criticalWarningDesc: These rules protect your bitcoins forever
seedLab.regenerateConfirm: Generate new seed? Current one will be lost (make sure you saved it if it was important!)
seedLab.labCompletedDesc: Now you understand why your seed phrase is your most valuable treasure 💎
seedLab.question: Question
seedLab.bip39Desc: BIP39 defines the standard for mnemonic codes. It uses 2048 carefully selected words from various languages to create human-readable backups of cryptographic keys.

merkleLab.title: Merkle Tree Lab
merkleLab.subtitle: Build and explore Merkle Trees like Bitcoin does
merkleLab.whyImportantDesc: Merkle Trees let you verify a single transaction without downloading 500GB+ of blockchain. This is how lightweight wallets like Electrum work!
merkleLab.selectLeaf: Select a leaf to generate proof
merkleLab.step2: Pair hashes and hash them together
merkleLab.step4: The final hash is the Merkle Root
merkleLab.whatIsMerkleDesc: A data structure that efficiently verifies data integrity using cryptographic hashes. Named after Ralph Merkle, 1979.
merkleLab.whyMerkleDesc: Allows SPV (Simplified Payment Verification) clients to verify transactions without downloading the entire blockchain.
merkleLab.spvDesc: With just the Merkle Root and a proof path, anyone can verify a transaction is in a block—no full node needed!
merkleLab.tip: Click any leaf to generate a Merkle Proof!
merkleLab.quizDesc: Prove you understand how Merkle Trees work
merkleLab.quizQuestion1: What is the main benefit of Merkle Trees in Bitcoin?
merkleLab.quizOption1B: They allow verification without downloading the full blockchain
merkleLab.quizCorrect1: ✅ Correct! SPV clients use Merkle proofs to verify transactions with minimal data.
merkleLab.quizIncorrect1: ❌ Not quite. Think about data efficiency and verification.
merkleLab.quizQuestion2: If you change ONE transaction in a block, what happens?
merkleLab.quizOption2B: The Merkle Root and all parent hashes change
merkleLab.quizCorrect2: ✅ Correct! Changing any leaf changes its parent, and so on up to the root. This is cryptographic integrity!
merkleLab.quizIncorrect2: ❌ Think about how hashes propagate upward in the tree.
merkleLab.quizComplete: 🎉 Quiz Complete! You understand Merkle Trees!
merkleLab.question: Question
merkleLab.labCompletedDesc: Now you understand how Bitcoin verifies transactions efficiently with Merkle Trees! 🌳
merkleLab.realWorldDesc: When your phone wallet shows "Transaction confirmed", it likely received just the Merkle Root + a small proof (~1KB) instead of the full block (~1-4MB). That's the power of Merkle!
merkleLab.securityNoteDesc: If an attacker tries to fake a transaction, they'd need to recalculate the Merkle Root and fool the entire network. The cryptographic chain makes this computationally impossible.
merkleLab.nodeTooltip: Leaf node: Represents one transaction. Click to generate a proof!
merkleLab.rootTooltip: Merkle Root: Single hash that represents ALL transactions in the block. Stored in the block header!
merkleLab.proofTooltip: Proof path: The sibling hashes needed to recompute the root. Only log₂(n) hashes needed!
merkleLab.oddTxNote: If odd number of transactions, the last one is duplicated.
merkleLab.doubleHashNote: Bitcoin uses double SHA-256: SHA256(SHA256(data)) for extra security.
merkleLab.efficiencyNote: For 1000 transactions, you only need ~10 hashes to prove inclusion. That's logarithmic efficiency!

miningLab.title: Mining Simulator Lab
miningLab.subtitle: Experience Proof-of-Work like Bitcoin miners do
miningLab.whyImportantDesc: Proof-of-Work is Bitcoin's security engine. Miners spend real energy to find valid hashes, making attacks economically impossible. Feel that energy yourself!
miningLab.difficultyDesc: More zeros = harder to find = more attempts needed
miningLab.labCompletedDesc: Now you understand how Bitcoin miners secure the network with real energy! ⚡
miningLab.quizDesc: Prove you understand how mining secures Bitcoin
miningLab.quizQuestion1: Why does Bitcoin mining use so much energy?
miningLab.quizOption1C: To generate electricity as a byproduct
miningLab.quizCorrect1: ✅ Correct! The energy cost makes it too expensive to attack the network. Security through economics!
miningLab.quizIncorrect1: ❌ Think about what would happen if mining was free and easy.
miningLab.quizQuestion2: What happens when more miners join the network?
miningLab.quizCorrect2: ✅ Correct! Bitcoin adjusts difficulty every 2016 blocks (~2 weeks) to keep block time at ~10 minutes.
miningLab.quizIncorrect2: ❌ Think about how Bitcoin maintains consistent block times.
miningLab.question: Question
miningLab.step1: Miners collect transactions into a block
miningLab.step2: They hash the block header with a changing nonce
miningLab.step3: If hash starts with enough zeros, block is valid!
miningLab.step4: Otherwise, change nonce and try again (billions of times!)
miningLab.whatIsPoWDesc: A consensus mechanism where miners compete to solve a cryptographic puzzle. The "work" is computational effort that's easy to verify but hard to produce.
miningLab.whyPoWDesc: PoW makes it economically irrational to attack the network. To rewrite history, you'd need more energy than all other miners combined!
miningLab.energyNoteDesc: Bitcoin's energy usage isn't waste—it's the price of decentralized, censorship-resistant money. Every joule spent secures your transactions.
miningLab.securityNoteDesc: The "longest chain" rule means the chain with the most cumulative work wins. This is why 51% attacks require controlling majority hash rate.
miningLab.funFactDesc: The Bitcoin network does ~500 exahashes (500,000,000,000,000,000,000) per second. Your phone just did a few thousand. That's the scale of Bitcoin security!
miningLab.tooltipDifficulty: Number of leading zeros required. Each extra zero makes it ~16x harder!
miningLab.tooltipHash: SHA-256 output: a unique 64-character fingerprint of your input. Change one bit, and the whole hash changes!
miningLab.tooltipNonce: "Number used once": the variable miners change to find a valid hash. Like guessing a combination lock!
miningLab.tooltipHashrate: Guesses per second your device can make. Higher = faster mining (but also more battery drain!).
miningLab.difficultyNormal: Normal mode - Like early Bitcoin days
miningLab.difficultyHard: Hard mode - Your device will work!
miningLab.difficultyExpert: Expert mode - This might take a while...

signingLab.title: Digital Signatures Lab
signingLab.subtitle: Learn how Bitcoin proves ownership without revealing secrets
signingLab.privateKeyDesc: Your secret password. Never share this!
signingLab.publicKeyDesc: Your public address. Safe to share.
signingLab.signatureDesc: Proof that you own the private key.
signingLab.analogyKeyDesc: Only you have it. It lets you unlock your money.
signingLab.analogyLockDesc: Anyone can see it. They use it to send you money.
signingLab.analogySealDesc: Proves you sent it. Cannot be faked!
signingLab.ecdsaSimpleDesc: Used since 2009. Secure, but slightly complex math.
signingLab.schnorrSimpleDesc: Added in 2021. Simpler, smaller, and smarter!
signingLab.schnorrUpgrade: Why Better? Smaller size + Better Privacy
signingLab.musigSimpleDesc: Alice + Bob + Carol = One combined signature!
signingLab.musigBenefit: Benefit: Looks like a normal transaction (Privacy!)
signingLab.nonceSimpleDesc: Every signature needs a unique random number (nonce).
signingLab.quizQuestion: What happens if you reuse a nonce?
